author: PJ
text: ah, why do we edit gitconfig? I don't like that, do we have to? I would prefer if we stored what we needed in say a .clair/ folder or something?

author: PJ
text: Want me to make the change (alias → `.git/clair/alias`, drop the gitconfig writes)? And hidden in `.git/` or visible at root?

author: JB
text: Want me to do that repackage now, or keep refining design first?

author: JB
text: Either way the change stands on its own merits (clair shouldn't touch your git config), and your instinct to test empirically rather than take my word is the right call. Run it and tell me what you see — if it still fails, the next move is to capture the *exact* failure with `ctrl+o` expanded on the tool call, because that raw error will tell us far more than the paraphrase has.
